STAG MOUNTAIN RAWS.

SPOTTER REPORTED WIND DAMAGE...WHICH WAS VERIFIED THROUGH LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT. AT LEAST ONE TREE WAS DOWN ON A HOUSE...MULTIPLE TREES EITHER SNAPPED OR UPROOTED...AND MINOR DAMAGE TO POWER POLES. NV ENERGY HAD TO WORK ON SOME LINES FROM THE STORM.

THE PUBLIC REPORTED WIND DAMAGE IN THE NORTHERN SECTIONS OF WMC. THIS WAS VERIFIED THROUGH LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT. AT LEAST ONE TREE FELL ON A HOUSE...MULTIPLE TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED...AND MINOR DAMAGE TO POWER POLES. NV ENERGY HAD TO MAKE MINOR REPAIRS.

FLOODING REPORTED BY HUMBOLDT COUNTY SHERIFF AT THE PARADISE RANCHES NEAR THE JUNCTION OF US HIGHWAY 95 AND STATE ROUTE 290.

SEVERAL REPORTS OF FLOODED ROADS...WASHED OUT ROADS...AND FLOODING OF PROPERTY. DAMAGE TO STEEL FENCES FROM DEBRIS FLOWS. OCCURRED BETWEEN 9 AND 11 PM.
PICTURES FROM NORTH OF TONOPAH SHOWED A WALL OF DUST MOVING IN. STRONG WINDS WERE ALSO REPORTED.
61 MPH WIND GUST AT WARM SPRINGS SUMMIT CEMP FROM THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW WINDS.
DUST STORM. VISIBILITY LOWERED MOSTLY TO 1 TO 2 MILES. IT BRIEFLY LOWERED TO LESS THAN ONE MILE IN THE EARLY MORNING.
ROUGHLY TEN POWER POLES SNAPPED DUE TO STRONG THUNDERSTORM WINDS...TIME ESTIMATED BASED OFF RADAR AND RECEIVED REPORTS.

MESONET STATION MOREY CREEK RAWS /MORN2/
A SHED WAS DESTROYED BY THE WIND. SHED HAD BEEN THERE FOR 11 YEARS AND WAS THROWN 130 FT...BEFORE BEING WRAPPED AROUND A TREE.
NUMEROUS REPORTS FROM SPOTTERS AND PUBLIC OF UP TO 6 INCH TREE BRANCHES BROKEN OFF
VERY HEAVY RAIN PRODUCED FLASH FLOODING. FISH CREEK OVERFLOWED ITS BANK AND WASHED ACROSS THE ROAD. WATER WAS AT LEAST 6 INCHES DEEP.
WATER AND DEBRIS OVER STATE ROUTE 306 BETWEEN INTERSTATE 80 AND THE RIVER NEAR BEOWAWE IN 4 DIFFERENT PLACES.
WATER FLOWING OVER ROAD MADE IT IMPASSABLE. LIGHTNING FROM STORM KILLED A COW.

RECEIVED MULTIPLE REPORTS OF FLOODING ON ROUTE 89 GABBS POLE LINE ROAD NEAR THE CRESCENT DUNES SOLAR ENERGY PLANT. FIRST REPORTS OF FLOODING AROUND 1:00 PM
TONOPAH AIRPORT MEASURED PEAK WIND OF 50 KNOTS. LIKELY OUTFLOW FROM NEARBY THUNDERSTORMS TO THE WEST
PUBLIC REPORTED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA QUARTER SIZE HAIL ROUGHLY 2 MILES WEST OF LAGES JUNCTION. TIME ESTIMATED BASED OFF RADAR.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORTS AND PICTURES OF FLOODING IN AND AROUND AUSTIN, NV. TIME ESTIMATED BASED OFF RADAR.

NEVADA DEPARTMENT OF WILDLIFE'S SPRING CREEK REARING STATION REPORTED A LOSS OF 1500 HEAD OF FISH AT THE HATCHERY FROM FLASH FLOODING RUNOFF OF THE SNAKE CREEK DUE TO HEAVY RAIN. RECORDED RAIN MEASUREMENT OF 1.35 IN A TWO HOUR SPAN FROM A NEARBY OBSERVER. ESTIMATED MONETARY LOSS OF $4,500-$5,000.
MULTIPLE REPORTS FROM SOCIAL MEDIA OF STREET FLOODING IN AND AROUND TONOPAH DUE TO HEAVY RAIN IN THE MORNING AND AFTERNOON. FIRST REPORTS OF FLOODING AT 6:50 AM.
THUNDERSTORM WITH SMALL HAIL AND SEVERE WIND GUSTS MOVED THROUGH ELY.
